question: Which of these statements is true?

answers.text: Bobtail tractors can take longer to stop than a combination vehicle loaded to maximum gross weight.
answers.explanation: Like an empty tractor-trailer combination, a bobtail takes longer to stop because the springs are unloaded and thus the brakes must be applied slower to avoid locking up the wheels.

answers.text: Light vehicles need more braking power to stop than heavy ones.
The brakes and suspension systems of combination vehicles work best with a light load.

explanation: Like an empty tractor-trailer combination, a bobtail takes longer to stop because the springs are unloaded and thus the brakes must be applied slower to avoid locking up the wheels.

question: If the service line comes apart while you are driving a combination vehicle, but the emergency line stays together, what will happen right away?

answers.text: The emergency trailer brakes will come on.
The trailer's tank will exhaust through the open line.
Nothing is likely to happen until you try to apply the brakes.
answers.explanation: When attempting to apply the brakes air loss from the leaking service line will quickly lower the air tank pressure. If the air pressure falls low enough the trailer emergency brakes will apply.

explanation: When attempting to apply the brakes air loss from the leaking service line will quickly lower the air tank pressure. If the air pressure falls low enough the trailer emergency brakes will apply.

question: Where are the air shut-off valves located on a trailer?

answers.text: At the front
On the left side
At the rear
answers.explanation: Trailer shut-off valves are located at the rear of the trailer.

explanation: Trailer shut-off valves are located at the rear of the trailer.

question: When uncoupling the trailer, after you shut off the trailer air supply and lock the trailer brakes, you should:

answers.text: Back up gently to ease pressure on the fifth wheel.
answers.explanation: After shutting off the trailer air supply and locking the trailer brakes, decrease the pressure on the 5th wheel by gently backing up so the wheel lock level can easily be released.

answers.text: Lower trailer landing gear.
Put on your tractor parking brakes.

explanation: After shutting off the trailer air supply and locking the trailer brakes, decrease the pressure on the 5th wheel by gently backing up so the wheel lock level can easily be released.

question: The front trailer supports are up and the trailer is resting on the tractor. Make sure:

answers.text: Both options are correct.
answers.explanation: Once the trailer is in position on the tractor, check for clearance between the landing gear, and tractor frame and the clearance between the top of the tractor tires and the nose of the trailer.

answers.text: There is enough clearance between the tractor frame and landing gear.
There is enough clearance between the top of the tractor tires and nose of the trailer.

explanation: Once the trailer is in position on the tractor, check for clearance between the landing gear, and tractor frame and the clearance between the top of the tractor tires and the nose of the trailer.

question: When you are uncoupling a loaded trailer, you should lower the landing gear until it:

answers.text: Reaches the ground.
Make firm contact with the ground, turn crank in low gear a few extra turns.
answers.explanation: Lift some of the weight off the tractor by turning the crank in low gear a few extra turns if the trailer is loaded and the landing gear is making contact with the ground.

answers.text: Make contact with the ground and leave the trailer off the fifth wheel.

explanation: Lift some of the weight off the tractor by turning the crank in low gear a few extra turns if the trailer is loaded and the landing gear is making contact with the ground.

question: Your emergency air line breaks or gets pulled apart while you are driving. The loss of pressure will cause the:

answers.text: Tractor's air to dump into the trailer tank through the service line.
Air compressor to unload instead of pumping air.
Emergency trailer brakes to come on.
answers.explanation: If all the air in the trailer is lost to a leak, the emergency brakes will be activated. Ensure that both air lines to the trailer are connected correctly and not leaking.

explanation: If all the air in the trailer is lost to a leak, the emergency brakes will be activated. Ensure that both air lines to the trailer are connected correctly and not leaking.

question: After you have coupled the trailer you should raise the landing gear by first using:

answers.text: High gear.
Low gear.
answers.explanation: If equipped, start by using the low gear range to raise the landing gear and then switch to high gear once it is free.

answers.text: Intermediate gear.

explanation: If equipped, start by using the low gear range to raise the landing gear and then switch to high gear once it is free.

question: Which trailer braking system will be activated if an air leak is severe enough to activate the tractor protection valve?

answers.text: Emergency brake system
answers.explanation: When pressure drops far enough for the tractor protection valve to activate, the trailer emergency (spring) brake will engage once air pressure can no longer hold them back.

answers.text: Parking brake system
Service brake system

explanation: When pressure drops far enough for the tractor protection valve to activate, the trailer emergency (spring) brake will engage once air pressure can no longer hold them back.

question: A driver crosses the air lines when hooking up to an old trailer. What will happen?

answers.text: The brake lights will come on when the brake pedal is pressed.
The hand valve will apply the tractor brakes instead of the trailer brakes.
If the trailer has no spring brakes, you could drive away but you would not have trailer brakes.
answers.explanation: This situation would be extremely dangerous as crossing the lines would result in not having any trailer brakes since pre-1975 trailers tend to not have spring brakes.

explanation: This situation would be extremely dangerous as crossing the lines would result in not having any trailer brakes since pre-1975 trailers tend to not have spring brakes.

question: Glad hands are usually connecting which of these?

answers.text: Service and emergency air lines from the vehicle to the trailer.
answers.explanation: Glad hands are couplers that use a rubber seal to prevent air from escaping when connecting the service and emergency air line between the truck/tractor and the trailer.

answers.text: Electric lines from the tractor to trailer.
Kingpin from the trailer to the locking jaws of the fifth wheel.

explanation: Glad hands are couplers that use a rubber seal to prevent air from escaping when connecting the service and emergency air line between the truck/tractor and the trailer.

question: You are about to back your tractor under a semitrailer. The trailer is at the right height when the:

answers.text: Trailer will be lifted slightly when the tractor backs under it.
answers.explanation: Adjust the trailer height so that the tractor should just slightly raise the trailer on the 5th wheel coupler when the truck backs under it.

answers.text: Kingpin is about 1 1/4 inches above the fifth wheel.
End of the kingpin is even with the top of the fifth wheel.

explanation: Adjust the trailer height so that the tractor should just slightly raise the trailer on the 5th wheel coupler when the truck backs under it.

question: When connecting the glad hands, press the two seals with the couplers together at what degree to each other?

answers.text: 45 degrees.
90 degrees.
answers.explanation: The two seals of the glad hands should be pressed together at a 90-degree angle to each other during connection.

answers.text: 180 degrees.

explanation: The two seals of the glad hands should be pressed together at a 90-degree angle to each other during connection.

question: Why should you lock the tractor glad hands to each other (or dummy couplers) when you are not towing a trailer?

answers.text: It will keep dirt or water out of the lines.
answers.explanation: Locking the tractor glad hands with each other when not pulling a trailer is the best way to avoid contamination from getting to the coupler and air supply system.

answers.text: If you did not, you could never build system pressure.
The connected brake circuit becomes a backup air tank.

explanation: Locking the tractor glad hands with each other when not pulling a trailer is the best way to avoid contamination from getting to the coupler and air supply system.

question: Which type of vehicle is LEAST likely to become stuck on a raised railroad crossing?

answers.text: Car carrier
Moving van
Cargo tanker
answers.explanation: Vehicles with low slung units (car carrier and moving vans) or single-axle tractors that are pulling a long trailer with the landing gear in the tandem-axle position are most likely to become stuck on a raised railroad crossing.

explanation: Vehicles with low slung units (car carrier and moving vans) or single-axle tractors that are pulling a long trailer with the landing gear in the tandem-axle position are most likely to become stuck on a raised railroad crossing.

question: All the following are true of antilock braking systems, EXCEPT:

answers.text: The vehicle will stop at a shorter distance
answers.explanation: ABS does not decrease the distance required to stop. It merely prevents the brakes from locking up so the driver can maintain control of the vehicle.

answers.text: ABS is a supplemental system to the foundation brakes
ABS only activates when a wheel is about to lock up

explanation: ABS does not decrease the distance required to stop. It merely prevents the brakes from locking up so the driver can maintain control of the vehicle.

question: The air brake leakage rate for a combination vehicle (engine off, brakes off) should not be more than ____ psi per minute.

answers.explanation: During a static leak test the air brakes should not lose more than 3 psi in 1 minute for a combination vehicle.

explanation: During a static leak test the air brakes should not lose more than 3 psi in 1 minute for a combination vehicle.

question: Backing a trailer alone, into a curved path toward the driver's side, would include each of the following, EXCEPT:

answers.text: Monitoring your progress in both mirrors
Getting out of the truck, and checking that the path is clear
Avoid pulling up and starting over for any reason
answers.explanation: When backing alone, get out and visually verify all locations and that the path is clear. Back up slowly and use mirrors to monitor your progress. Pull forward to reposition if necessary.

explanation: When backing alone, get out and visually verify all locations and that the path is clear. Back up slowly and use mirrors to monitor your progress. Pull forward to reposition if necessary.

question: Why should you be sure that the fifth wheel plate is greased as required?

answers.text: To prevent steering problems.
answers.explanation: Friction between the tractor and trailer at the 5th wheel coupler can be caused by a lack of lubrication resulting in steering issues. Keep the 5th wheel plate adequately greased at all times.

answers.text: To reduce heat and noise.
To ensure a good electrical connection.

explanation: Friction between the tractor and trailer at the 5th wheel coupler can be caused by a lack of lubrication resulting in steering issues. Keep the 5th wheel plate adequately greased at all times.

question: Sudden turns or aggressive braking will have the greatest effect on which trailer?

answers.text: The front trailer
The middle trailer
The rear trailer
answers.explanation: Any sudden movements or aggressive braking will always have a more profound effect on the rear most trailer causing it to get out of line or even rollover.

explanation: Any sudden movements or aggressive braking will always have a more profound effect on the rear most trailer causing it to get out of line or even rollover.

question: What position should the landing gear be in prior to starting a trip?

answers.text: Raised completely
answers.explanation: To avoid any issues when crossing railroad tracks, ensure the landing gear is raised completely before starting a trip.

answers.text: Raised halfway
One foot off the ground

explanation: To avoid any issues when crossing railroad tracks, ensure the landing gear is raised completely before starting a trip.

question: When backing under the trailer to begin coupling, from which direction should you approach?

answers.text: Directly in front of the trailer lining up the driver's side truck wheels with the trailer wheels
On a 45-degree angle to the trailer lining up the fifth wheel with the kingpin.
Directly in front of the trailer lining up the fifth wheel with the kingpin.
answers.explanation: it is important to back from directly in front of the trailer as you line up the fifth wheel with the kingpin. Do not hit the trailer and try not to hit the kingpin hard enough to move the trailer or bend the kingpin.

explanation: it is important to back from directly in front of the trailer as you line up the fifth wheel with the kingpin. Do not hit the trailer and try not to hit the kingpin hard enough to move the trailer or bend the kingpin.

question: Which task is NOT performed during the coupling process after having locked the trailer onto the tractor and secured the vehicle?

answers.text: Check the trailer height
Connect air lines to the trailer
Verifying that the shut-off valves are closed.
answers.explanation: Once the vehicle has been secured, the next processes are to check the trailer height and connect the air lines to the trailer.

explanation: Once the vehicle has been secured, the next processes are to check the trailer height and connect the air lines to the trailer.

question: The safety latch for the fifth wheel locking level must be ____ for a coupling to be complete.

answers.text: Over the locking lever
answers.explanation: The safety latch for the fifth wheel locking level must be under the locking lever for coupling to be complete. On some 5th wheels, it may need to be moved by hand.

answers.text: Straight up
Under the locking lever

explanation: The safety latch for the fifth wheel locking level must be under the locking lever for coupling to be complete. On some 5th wheels, it may need to be moved by hand.

question: If only the trailer is equipped with antilock brakes, and the tractor is without ABS, what may be the result?

answers.text: The trailer is more likely to remain straight behind the tractor during hard braking.
answers.explanation: In the event that only the trailer has ABS, then under hard braking the wheels will not lock up and the trailer is more likely to remain straight behind the tractor.

answers.text: The trailer is more likely to cause jackknifing.
The trailer is more likely to skid and turn sideways during hard braking.

explanation: In the event that only the trailer has ABS, then under hard braking the wheels will not lock up and the trailer is more likely to remain straight behind the tractor.

question: When uncoupling a fully loaded trailer, which task should be accomplished right after the landing gear has contacted the ground?

answers.text: Lift the trailer partially off the tractor.
answers.explanation: For a fully loaded trailer, the next step should be to partially lift the load off the tractor.

answers.text: Lift the trailer completely off the tractor.
Secure the crank handle of the lift

explanation: For a fully loaded trailer, the next step should be to partially lift the load off the tractor.

question: Should the trailer hand brake be applied when a trailer starts to jackknife?

answers.text: Yes, applying the brake will help to straighten the trailer.
No, the brakes need to be released to help straighten the trailer.
answers.explanation: When a wheel is locked up and in a skid, the brakes must be released to allow the wheel to turn and straighten the vehicle. Therefore, any brakes to the trailer need to be released and not applied.

answers.text: Yes, the brakes will help stop the trailer before it hits the tractor.

explanation: When a wheel is locked up and in a skid, the brakes must be released to allow the wheel to turn and straighten the vehicle. Therefore, any brakes to the trailer need to be released and not applied.

question: A purpose of the service line is to:

answers.text: apply the parking brakes of the trailer.
send air to relay valves to apply the trailer brakes.
answers.explanation: Air from the service line is controlled by the brake pedal and the trailer hand brake. Relay valves are attached to the service line to apply the trailer brakes quickly when the brake pedal is pressed.

answers.text: apply the emergency brakes on the trailer when air pressure is lost.

explanation: Air from the service line is controlled by the brake pedal and the trailer hand brake. Relay valves are attached to the service line to apply the trailer brakes quickly when the brake pedal is pressed.

question: What is the root cause of over half of CDL operator deaths?

answers.text: CMV to CMV collisions
Vehicle rollovers
answers.explanation: CMV rollovers are the primary cause of over half the CDL operator deaths in the US.

answers.text: Hazardous materials-related accidents

explanation: CMV rollovers are the primary cause of over half the CDL operator deaths in the US.

question: Where should the tractor be when you inspect the landing gear after uncoupling the trailer?

answers.text: With the fifth wheel directly beneath the kingpin.
With the tractor frame under the trailer.
answers.explanation: Steps 7 through 9. Stop with the tractor frame under the trailer. Place the transmission in neutral and set the parking brake. Exit the tractor and inspect the landing gear to ensure it has not failed nor has it sunk into the ground.

answers.text: Completely cleared from the trailer.

explanation: Steps 7 through 9. Stop with the tractor frame under the trailer. Place the transmission in neutral and set the parking brake. Exit the tractor and inspect the landing gear to ensure it has not failed nor has it sunk into the ground.

question: A trailer has a maximum weight rating of 30,000 pounds. Which weight load is more likely to result in a jackknife?

answers.text: 30,000 pounds
20,000 pounds
10,000 pounds
answers.explanation: Braking systems are designed to perform best under full load. Lower weights make it easier to lock up the wheels. Also, more weight results in better traction during braking.

explanation: Braking systems are designed to perform best under full load. Lower weights make it easier to lock up the wheels. Also, more weight results in better traction during braking.

question: Under which condition would you use wheel chocks to park a trailer that does not have spring brakes?

answers.text: Not under any condition
Under all conditions
answers.explanation: If an older trailer lacks spring brakes, always use wheel chocks to hold the vehicle from rolling in case the parking brakes lose air pressure.

answers.text: Only if the parking brakes have failed

explanation: If an older trailer lacks spring brakes, always use wheel chocks to hold the vehicle from rolling in case the parking brakes lose air pressure.

question: You are coupling a tractor to a semitrailer and have backed up, but are not under it. What should you hook up before backing under?

answers.text: The emergency and service air lines.
answers.explanation: Connect both the service air and emergency lines to the trailer prior to backing under the trailer.

answers.text: The electrical service cable.
Nothing. Back up and lock the fifth wheel.

explanation: Connect both the service air and emergency lines to the trailer prior to backing under the trailer.

question: A trailer has a maximum weight rating of 28,000 pounds. Which weight load is more likely to result in a rollover?

answers.text: 10,000 pounds
16,000 pounds
28,000 pounds
answers.explanation: A fully loaded trailer is 10 times more likely to rollover during an accident.

explanation: A fully loaded trailer is 10 times more likely to rollover during an accident.

question: After you supply air to the trailer, make sure the air lines are not crossed and the trailer brakes are working. This is done by:

answers.text: Watching your mirrors to see if the trailer lights come on.
Lifting the brake pedal.
Applying and releasing the trailer brakes and listening for the sound of the brake.
answers.explanation: Checking for crossed-up air service and emergency lines on a trailer can easily be accomplished by applying and releasing the brakes and listening for them to activate and the air to be released from the line.

explanation: Checking for crossed-up air service and emergency lines on a trailer can easily be accomplished by applying and releasing the brakes and listening for them to activate and the air to be released from the line.

question: In normal driving, some drivers use the hand valve before the brake pedal to prevent a jack-knife. Which of the statements is true?

answers.text: It results in less skidding than using the brake pedal alone.
It should not be done.
answers.explanation: The hand valve should only be used when testing the trailer brakes and not while driving as it may cause a skid. The valve is sometimes referred to as the Johnson bar or trolley valve.

answers.text: It is the best way to brake and keep the truck in a straight line.

explanation: The hand valve should only be used when testing the trailer brakes and not while driving as it may cause a skid. The valve is sometimes referred to as the Johnson bar or trolley valve.

question: Which of the following will help monitor any potential hazards in the roadway ahead?

answers.text: Vehicle mirrors
The GPS system
Monitoring the roadway 12 to 15 seconds ahead
answers.explanation: Use your sight, hearing, smell, and feel to detect any possible issues. Monitor the roadway for potential hazards 12 to 15 seconds down the road.

explanation: Use your sight, hearing, smell, and feel to detect any possible issues. Monitor the roadway for potential hazards 12 to 15 seconds down the road.

question: You are coupling a tractor to the semitrailer; you have connected the airlines. Before backing under the trailer, you should:

answers.text: Supply air to the trailer system, then pull out the air supply knob.
answers.explanation: At this point it is necessary to supply air to the trailer brake system by pushing in the air supply knob or moving the tractor protection valve control from the emergency to the normal position.

answers.text: Pull ahead to test the glad hands connection.
Make sure that the trailer brakes are off.

explanation: At this point it is necessary to supply air to the trailer brake system by pushing in the air supply knob or moving the tractor protection valve control from the emergency to the normal position.

question: In which position should the electrical cables be placed during uncoupling?

answers.text: Coiled with a cap on the end of the plug
With the plug facing down
answers.explanation: To help with keeping moisture out of the electrical cable it should be hung up with the end facing down.

answers.text: With the plug facing up

explanation: To help with keeping moisture out of the electrical cable it should be hung up with the end facing down.

question: What task is performed once the kingpin is locked into the fifth wheel and the front trailer supports are up so the trailer sits on the fifth wheel?

answers.text: Check for space between the upper and lower fifth wheel
Check for adequate clearance to turn between the tractor frame and trailer
answers.explanation: With the trailer sitting on the fifth wheel and the front trailer supports raised, check for adequate clearance for turning between the tractor frame front of the trailer.

answers.text: Place the safety latch in the unlocked position

explanation: With the trailer sitting on the fifth wheel and the front trailer supports raised, check for adequate clearance for turning between the tractor frame front of the trailer.

question: What is the most common cause of a tractor-trailer jackknife?

answers.text: A skid on the front steering wheels
Rear drive-wheel skid on the tractor
answers.explanation: The most common cause of jackknifing is a rear drive-wheel skid on the tractor. The back of the tractor begins to move sideways during the skid and the weight of the trailer then pushes forward compounding the issue.

answers.text: A trailer wheel skid

explanation: The most common cause of jackknifing is a rear drive-wheel skid on the tractor. The back of the tractor begins to move sideways during the skid and the weight of the trailer then pushes forward compounding the issue.

question: Air brake equipped trailers made before 1975:

answers.text: Cannot be legally operated on interstate highways.
Are not required to have spring brakes.
answers.explanation: Prior to 1975 trailers and converter dollies were not required to have spring brakes. Post-1975 trailers will all have spring brakes.

answers.text: Usually need a glad hand converter.

explanation: Prior to 1975 trailers and converter dollies were not required to have spring brakes. Post-1975 trailers will all have spring brakes.

question: Which combination of endorsements is required to drive a triple combination?

answers.text: Class A (N)
Class A (S)
Class A (T)
answers.explanation: Class A CDL with (T) endorsement will be required in states where it is legal.

explanation: Class A CDL with (T) endorsement will be required in states where it is legal.

question: When you get ready to back under a semitrailer you should line up:

answers.text: The left rear outer dual wheel with the kingpin.
The kingpin to engage the driver's side locking jaw first.
Directly in front of the trailer.
answers.explanation: Backing into a trailer at an angle can cause the landing gear to break. Always back up directly in front of the trailer to ensure proper alignment and connection.

explanation: Backing into a trailer at an angle can cause the landing gear to break. Always back up directly in front of the trailer to ensure proper alignment and connection.

question: If the spring brakes are not released when you pushed the trailer air supply valve you should:

answers.text: Check air line connection.
answers.explanation: The spring brakes should release when the trailer air supply valve is pushed. If the brakes do not release inspect the supply line connections.

answers.text: Cross the airlines.
Check electrical cable.

explanation: The spring brakes should release when the trailer air supply valve is pushed. If the brakes do not release inspect the supply line connections.

question: Air and electrical lines from the tractor to the trailer should be:

answers.text: Resting on the frame of the tractor.
Secured but with enough loop to turn.
answers.explanation: Air and electrical lines should be connected and firmly secured in a loop with enough length to make a full turn with the tractor and trailer.

answers.text: Fully tight but away a little looped.

explanation: Air and electrical lines should be connected and firmly secured in a loop with enough length to make a full turn with the tractor and trailer.

question: While checking if the trailer is securely coupled to the tractor, the landing gear should be:

answers.text: Fully lowered.
Slightly raised.
answers.explanation: The landing gear should be slightly raised when checking if the trailer is securely coupled to the tractor.

answers.text: Fully raised.

explanation: The landing gear should be slightly raised when checking if the trailer is securely coupled to the tractor.

question: When making a right turn, what is the safest way to start a turn without entering the left lane at all?

answers.text: Turn sharply at the start of the turn (turn early)
Turn wide toward the end of the turn (turn later)
answers.explanation: Turning wide early may cause the following traffic to believe you are turning left. Start the turn later so you turn wide at the end of the turn.

answers.text: Turn normally and allow the trailer to cut across the curb at the corner of the intersection

explanation: Turning wide early may cause the following traffic to believe you are turning left. Start the turn later so you turn wide at the end of the turn.

question: Off-tracking or cheating causes which of these to follow the wider path while making a turn?

answers.text: 53-feet bobtail.
Tractor towing a 45-feet trailer.
Tractor with two 27-feet trailers.
answers.explanation: When a vehicle makes a turn the arc (path) taken by each wheel is different. The path is less noticeable on short passenger vehicles while being much more pronounced on long vehicles. Off-tracking is a method to ensure that the path of the rear wheels of the trailer can clear the turn of any obstacles that may be near, such as vehicles sitting at an intersection. The wheels of the furthest trailer will off-track the greatest amount.

explanation: When a vehicle makes a turn the arc (path) taken by each wheel is different. The path is less noticeable on short passenger vehicles while being much more pronounced on long vehicles. Off-tracking is a method to ensure that the path of the rear wheels of the trailer can clear the turn of any obstacles that may be near, such as vehicles sitting at an intersection. The wheels of the furthest trailer will off-track the greatest amount.

question: The higher the center of gravity of your truck, the:

answers.text: Easier to turn over.
answers.explanation: Keeping the cargo as low to the ground as possible lowers the center of gravity and promotes stability. The higher the center of gravity the greater the chance of a rollover.

answers.text: Easier to turn around the corner.
More stable while turning.

explanation: Keeping the cargo as low to the ground as possible lowers the center of gravity and promotes stability. The higher the center of gravity the greater the chance of a rollover.

question: To unlock the fifth wheel, the locking lever should be placed in the ____ position.

answers.text: Closed
Open
answers.explanation: Unlocking of the 5th wheel is done by placing the locking lever in the open position.

answers.text: Neutral

explanation: Unlocking of the 5th wheel is done by placing the locking lever in the open position.

question: Which task is NOT performed when verifying that air pressure is available to all trailers?

answers.text: Activating the tractor protection valve
answers.explanation: To validate that air pressure is available to all trailers, start by waiting for air pressure to reach operating pressure. Press the trailer air supply control knob, then open the emergency line shut-off valve at the last trailer and listen for air pressure leaking out.

answers.text: Pressing the trailer air supply control knob
Opening the emergency line shut-off valve

explanation: To validate that air pressure is available to all trailers, start by waiting for air pressure to reach operating pressure. Press the trailer air supply control knob, then open the emergency line shut-off valve at the last trailer and listen for air pressure leaking out.

question: After connecting the air lines but before backing under the trailer you should:

answers.text: Make sure that the trailer brakes are off.
Supply air to the trailer system by pushing in the air supply knob, then pull out the trailer air supply knob to set the brakes.
answers.explanation: At this point, it is necessary to supply air to the trailer brake system by pushing in the air supply knob or moving the tractor protection valve control from the emergency to the normal position.

answers.text: Walk around the rig to be sure that it is clear.

explanation: At this point, it is necessary to supply air to the trailer brake system by pushing in the air supply knob or moving the tractor protection valve control from the emergency to the normal position.

question: What will most likely result from activating the trailer hand valve if a trailer is jackknifing?

answers.text: The trailer will straighten out.
The trailer will continue to skid and jackknife.
answers.explanation: If the trailer is already skidding into a jackknife, then applying the trailer hand valve will lock up the wheels and continue the skid. The best action would be to let off the brakes to let the wheels turn and straighten out the trailer.

answers.text: The trailer will immediately stop.

explanation: If the trailer is already skidding into a jackknife, then applying the trailer hand valve will lock up the wheels and continue the skid. The best action would be to let off the brakes to let the wheels turn and straighten out the trailer.

question: When driving a set of doubles, it is necessary to close which shut-off valve in the last trailer:

answers.text: Back valve.
answers.explanation: Each shut-off valve should be in the open position, except the furthest one back on the last trailer being towed. The valves, or cut-out cocks, allow air to be turned off for the service and supply lines when other trailers are not in tow.

answers.text: Front valve.
Middle section valve.

explanation: Each shut-off valve should be in the open position, except the furthest one back on the last trailer being towed. The valves, or cut-out cocks, allow air to be turned off for the service and supply lines when other trailers are not in tow.

question: When is an antilock brake system the most useful

answers.text: Only on the tractor
Only on the trailer
On either the tractor or trailer
answers.explanation: Antilock brakes will be useful on both the tractor and trailer. Even if only one has ABS it will assist in maintaining control when braking.

explanation: Antilock brakes will be useful on both the tractor and trailer. Even if only one has ABS it will assist in maintaining control when braking.

question: To stop a trailer skid you should:

answers.text: Release the brakes.
answers.explanation: If a trailer skid is being caused by the brakes being applied, then release the brakes so the wheels may roll freely and straighten out the trailer. Since there are no drive wheels on a trailer, brake lock-up should be the only thing causing a trailer skid.

answers.text: Counter-steer.
Use the trailer hand brakes.

explanation: If a trailer skid is being caused by the brakes being applied, then release the brakes so the wheels may roll freely and straighten out the trailer. Since there are no drive wheels on a trailer, brake lock-up should be the only thing causing a trailer skid.

question: All the following are functions of the emergency air line, EXCEPT:

answers.text: The line provides air to the trailer air tanks
Controls emergency brakes on a combination vehicle
Controls the relay valves for the foot brake
answers.explanation: The emergency air line provides air to the trailer air tanks while controlling the emergency brakes on a combination vehicle

explanation: The emergency air line provides air to the trailer air tanks while controlling the emergency brakes on a combination vehicle

question: The combination of which two things will greatly reduce the likelihood of a rollover?

answers.text: Loading cargo as low as possible and slowing down for turns.
answers.explanation: Loading the cargo as low as possible to reduce the center of gravity combined with slowing down for turns will greatly reduce the likelihood of a rollover.

answers.text: Loading cargo on the axles and traveling slowly.
Loading cargo with a high center of gravity and slowing down for turns.

explanation: Loading the cargo as low as possible to reduce the center of gravity combined with slowing down for turns will greatly reduce the likelihood of a rollover.

question: You have a major leak in the service line, and you put on the brakes. Service air will escape and cause the:

answers.text: Trailer tank pressure to be lost.
Trailer emergency brakes to come on.
answers.explanation: When attempting to apply the brakes, air loss from the leaking service line will quickly lower the air tank pressure. If the air pressure falls low enough the trailer emergency brakes will apply.

answers.text: Tractor spring brakes to lock up.

explanation: When attempting to apply the brakes, air loss from the leaking service line will quickly lower the air tank pressure. If the air pressure falls low enough the trailer emergency brakes will apply.

question: Which task needs to be performed first when coupling a tractor to the trailer?

answers.text: Secure the truck
Inspect the trailer height
Verify the position of the fifth wheel
answers.explanation: First, verify the position of the fifth wheel so it is positioned to back into the kingpin.

explanation: First, verify the position of the fifth wheel so it is positioned to back into the kingpin.

question: There are two things that a driver can do to prevent a rollover. They are: (1) go slow around turns, and (2):

answers.text: Keep the fifth wheel free play as small as possible.
Keep the cargo as close to the ground as possible.
answers.explanation: Rollover prevention is primarily the result of going slow through turns (slowing momentum) and keeping the cargo as low to the ground as possible (lowering the center of gravity).

answers.text: Make sure that the brakes are properly balanced.

explanation: Rollover prevention is primarily the result of going slow through turns (slowing momentum) and keeping the cargo as low to the ground as possible (lowering the center of gravity).

question: When a trailer is equipped with ABS, where will the ABS malfunction indicator be located?

answers.text: On the trailer nose
Either the front or rear right corner
Either the front or rear left corner
answers.explanation: When a trailer is equipped with ABS it will have a malfunction indicator on either the front or rear left corner.

explanation: When a trailer is equipped with ABS it will have a malfunction indicator on either the front or rear left corner.

question: What other names may be used when referring to the service air line?

answers.text: The signal line, or the control line.
answers.explanation: At times, publications or people in the industry may call the service air line a control line, or the signal line.

answers.text: The emergency line, or the signal line.
The brake line, or the controlled line.

explanation: At times, publications or people in the industry may call the service air line a control line, or the signal line.

question: Failure to properly maintain the fifth wheel plate with grease may result in:

answers.text: Heat build-up from friction
Steering issues from friction
answers.explanation: The fifth wheel plate needs to have adequate grease to avoid any steering issues from friction.

answers.text: An inadequate ground to the tractor for electrical connections.

explanation: The fifth wheel plate needs to have adequate grease to avoid any steering issues from friction.

question: Each of the following may be used to keep the vehicle from moving when unattended, EXCEPT:

answers.text: Trailer hand valve
answers.explanation: When the vehicle will be unattended, the parking brake should be set, and optional wheel chocks can be placed. The trailer hand valve should only be used during testing.

answers.text: Wheel chocks
Parking brake

explanation: When the vehicle will be unattended, the parking brake should be set, and optional wheel chocks can be placed. The trailer hand valve should only be used during testing.

question: What is the primary issue of low-slung trailers?

answers.text: Increased chance of rolling over
Increased chance of getting stuck on a raised railroad crossing
answers.explanation: Low-slung trailers have poor clearance between the road and underside of the trailer. This results in an increased chance of getting the trailer stuck on a raised railroad crossing.

answers.text: Increased chance of a jackknife

explanation: Low-slung trailers have poor clearance between the road and underside of the trailer. This results in an increased chance of getting the trailer stuck on a raised railroad crossing.

question: The "crack-the-whip" effect is related to:

answers.text: The amount of off-tracking exhibited by the last trailer of a combination vehicle.
The susceptibility of the last trailer of a combination vehicle to rollover due to magnifying any vehicle movement.
answers.explanation: The "crack-the-whip" effect is related to the tendency of the last (or third) trailer of a combination vehicle to rollover the easiest because steering and braking movements are magnified further back on the vehicle.

answers.text: Skidding that is started in the front trailer being transferred to the rear trailer.

explanation: The "crack-the-whip" effect is related to the tendency of the last (or third) trailer of a combination vehicle to rollover the easiest because steering and braking movements are magnified further back on the vehicle.

question: Which of the following are provided air by the emergency line?

answers.text: The parking brakes
The tractor protection valve
The trailer air tanks
answers.explanation: The emergency air line to the trailer is also the supply line, providing air to the air tanks. If the line loses pressure, it will activate the emergency spring brakes.

explanation: The emergency air line to the trailer is also the supply line, providing air to the air tanks. If the line loses pressure, it will activate the emergency spring brakes.

question: Which device(s) controls air to the trailer service line?

answers.text: The trailer hand valve
The foot brake pedal
Both the trailer hand valve and foot brake pedal
answers.explanation: Both the trailer hand valve and foot brake pedal control air to the trailer service line on a combination vehicle.

explanation: Both the trailer hand valve and foot brake pedal control air to the trailer service line on a combination vehicle.

question: In which position should the tractor protection valve be placed when coupling a tractor-trailer?

answers.text: lever down
pulled out
Normal / pushed in
answers.explanation: The knob should be pushed in. On an older system, it should be in the "normal" position. On an old lever system, this would be UP.

explanation: The knob should be pushed in. On an older system, it should be in the "normal" position. On an old lever system, this would be UP.

question: When conducting the final air brake check for a three-trailer combination vehicle, where should the final air leak check be made?

answers.text: At the front trailer
At the middle trailer
At the rear trailer
answers.explanation: It is important to complete air brake checks for all vehicles to ensure that you have air pressure for braking. The last check will be done at the rear trailer.

explanation: It is important to complete air brake checks for all vehicles to ensure that you have air pressure for braking. The last check will be done at the rear trailer.

question: Which task is NOT part of the walkaround inspection of a combination vehicle, section 6.5.1?

answers.text: Inspect the coupling system areas
Inspect the landing gear
Lubricate the drawbar eye
answers.explanation: Although lubrication of the drawbar eye may be done at other times, it is not included for the walkaround inspection of a combination in section 6.5.1.

explanation: Although lubrication of the drawbar eye may be done at other times, it is not included for the walkaround inspection of a combination in section 6.5.1.

question: Prior to testing the tractor protection valve, in which position should the air supply control knob be placed?

answers.text: Pulled Out (Emergency)
answers.explanation: The air supply control knob should be in the pulled out (Emergency) position to test the tractor protection valve.

answers.text: Pushed In (Normal)
Pushed In (Emergency)

explanation: The air supply control knob should be in the pulled out (Emergency) position to test the tractor protection valve.

question: A tractor with a ____ trailer, requires the most amount of stopping distance.

answers.text: Lightly loaded
Fully loaded
Empty
answers.explanation: An empty tractor-trailer combination takes longer to stop because the springs are unloaded and thus the brakes must be applied slower to avoid locking up the wheels, resulting in a longer stopping period.

explanation: An empty tractor-trailer combination takes longer to stop because the springs are unloaded and thus the brakes must be applied slower to avoid locking up the wheels, resulting in a longer stopping period.

question: Purposes of the trailer emergency air lines include all the following, EXCEPT:

answers.text: To supply air to the trailer air tank
To release the parking brakes on the trailer
answers.explanation: The emergency air line provides air to the trailer air tank and activates the emergency brakes whenever air pressure is lost.

answers.text: To activate the trailer emergency brakes when air pressure is lost

explanation: The emergency air line provides air to the trailer air tank and activates the emergency brakes whenever air pressure is lost.

question: Which statement is true of turning while hauling a long trailer combination.

answers.text: When turning right, swing into the left lane to reduce off-tracking before turning back to the right.
When turning right, turn as wide as possible while remaining in your lane and keep the rear close to the curb.
answers.explanation: When turning right, turn as wide as possible while remaining in your lane and keep the rear close to the curb. You can turn late if traffic is clear in the lane next to your destination lane is clear. Do not swing into the left lane before starting a turn as traffic may falsely believe you are turning to the left.

answers.text: When turning left, turn early to cross the other traffic lanes as quickly as possible.

explanation: When turning right, turn as wide as possible while remaining in your lane and keep the rear close to the curb. You can turn late if traffic is clear in the lane next to your destination lane is clear. Do not swing into the left lane before starting a turn as traffic may falsely believe you are turning to the left.

question: When checking the trailer emergency brakes, the tractor protection control valve should be placed in what position?

answers.text: Emergency
answers.explanation: To check the trailer emergency brakes, ensure that the tractor protection control valve (aka tractor protection valve control or trailer emergency valve) is in the emergency position. Pull forward slowly to check that the emergency brakes are active.

answers.text: Normal
Neutral

explanation: To check the trailer emergency brakes, ensure that the tractor protection control valve (aka tractor protection valve control or trailer emergency valve) is in the emergency position. Pull forward slowly to check that the emergency brakes are active.

question: How much space should be allowed between the upper and lower fifth wheel after coupling?

answers.text: 1/4 inch.
Just enough to sit on.
None.
answers.explanation: Once coupled there should be absolutely no space between the upper and lower fifth wheel coupling. Any space indicates that the coupling was not successful and there is a risk of the trailer coming loose of the 5th wheel.

explanation: Once coupled there should be absolutely no space between the upper and lower fifth wheel coupling. Any space indicates that the coupling was not successful and there is a risk of the trailer coming loose of the 5th wheel.

question: What is the correct protocol to follow when your trailer gets stuck on a railroad crossing?

answers.text: Radio your dispatcher for further instructions.
Detach the tractor from the trailer and pull forward to clear the area, then contact emergency services.
Evacuate the vehicle. Call 911 or the appropriate emergency contact.
answers.explanation: When a trailer becomes stuck on a railroad crossing evacuate the vehicle then call 911 or the appropriate emergency contact.

explanation: When a trailer becomes stuck on a railroad crossing evacuate the vehicle then call 911 or the appropriate emergency contact.

question: Under which situation might the trailer hand valve be used as a parking brake for a combination vehicle?

answers.text: When the tractor protection valve has been activated.
When parking a full load on a steep grade.
No situation will allow the trailer hand valve to be used for parking.
answers.explanation: Only use the trailer hand valve for testing the trailer brakes. Under no circumstances should the valve be used as a parking brake.

explanation: Only use the trailer hand valve for testing the trailer brakes. Under no circumstances should the valve be used as a parking brake.

question: Compared to a straight truck or bus, there are ____ things to inspect in combination vehicles.

answers.text: More
answers.explanation: A combination vehicle has more items to inspect than a single vehicle, plus additional technology not found on single vehicles. Refer to the 7-step inspection procedure to properly inspect the vehicle.

answers.text: The same number
Fewer

explanation: A combination vehicle has more items to inspect than a single vehicle, plus additional technology not found on single vehicles. Refer to the 7-step inspection procedure to properly inspect the vehicle.

question: If you cannot complete your turn without entering another traffic lane you should:

answers.text: Turn wide before you start the turn.
Turn wide as you complete the turn.
answers.explanation: If you cannot use an off-track maneuver to make a turn, then turn as wide as possible by allowing the truck to move as far forward as possible before starting to turn to give more room for the trailer to clear the turn.

answers.text: Not make a turn and move to another place where you can make a turn without crossing other lane.

explanation: If you cannot use an off-track maneuver to make a turn, then turn as wide as possible by allowing the truck to move as far forward as possible before starting to turn to give more room for the trailer to clear the turn.

question: The air lines on a combination vehicle are often coloured to prevent them from mixing up. The emergency line is ____, the service line is ____.

answers.text: blue, red
red, blue
answers.explanation: The service and emergency air lines are color coded to prevent them from being mixed up during connection. The service line will be blue while the emergency line is red.

answers.text: black, yellow

explanation: The service and emergency air lines are color coded to prevent them from being mixed up during connection. The service line will be blue while the emergency line is red.

question: The air leakage rate for a combination vehicle (engine off, brakes on) should not be more than ____ psi per minute.

answers.explanation: During an applied leakage test the air brakes should not lose more than 4 psi in 1 minute for a combination of two vehicles.

explanation: During an applied leakage test the air brakes should not lose more than 4 psi in 1 minute for a combination of two vehicles.

question: Which of the following is to be expected when the tractor is backed under the trailer during coupling?

answers.text: The trailer will be slightly lifted onto the fifth wheel surface.
answers.explanation: It should be expected that the trailer will be slightly raised onto the fifth wheel surface as the kingpin slides into place.

answers.text: The trailer is likely to roll until the kingpin is in the fifth wheel.
The tractor frame may bump the front of the trailer when backing.

explanation: It should be expected that the trailer will be slightly raised onto the fifth wheel surface as the kingpin slides into place.

question: The iron trailer supports are up and the trailer is resting on the tractor. Make sure:

answers.text: The safety latch is in unlocked position.
There is enough clearance between the upper and lower fifth wheels.
There is enough clearance between the tractor frame and the landing gear.
answers.explanation: Ensure that there is adequate clearance between the rear of the tractor frame and the landing gear prior to pulling forward with the tractor.

explanation: Ensure that there is adequate clearance between the rear of the tractor frame and the landing gear prior to pulling forward with the tractor.

question: Which task is NOT performed during the tractor protection valve test?

answers.text: Turning off the ignition.
Venting air to the atmosphere by applying and releasing the brake pedal.
Accelerating to 5 mph and then applying the brakes
answers.explanation: Once the air brake pressure is up to operating pressure, turn off the ignition. Pump the service brake to vent air to the atmosphere, the tractor protection valve should activate when pressure reaches 20 to 45 psi.

explanation: Once the air brake pressure is up to operating pressure, turn off the ignition. Pump the service brake to vent air to the atmosphere, the tractor protection valve should activate when pressure reaches 20 to 45 psi.

question: Which protentional issue is most likely to result from trying to couple the tractor-trailer with the trailer too high?

answers.text: Coupling may be incorrect with the kingpin in the wrong place on the fifth wheel
answers.explanation: If the trailer is too high, then the coupling may be incorrect as the kingpin will be out of position in the upper and lower fifth wheel plates.

answers.text: The trailer nose may be damaged when backing into the trailer
The trailer height will be off when the landing gear is raised

explanation: If the trailer is too high, then the coupling may be incorrect as the kingpin will be out of position in the upper and lower fifth wheel plates.

question: You have coupled with a semitrailer, where should you put the iron trailer supports before driving away?

answers.text: 3 turns off the top with the crank handle secured in its bracket.
Raised 1/2 way with the crank handle secured in its bracket.
Fully raised with the crank handle secured in its bracket.
answers.explanation: Raise the landing gear completely and place the crank handle in its bracket. Driving with the landing gear partially raised invites damage to the trailer if it catches on anything.

explanation: Raise the landing gear completely and place the crank handle in its bracket. Driving with the landing gear partially raised invites damage to the trailer if it catches on anything.

question: Application of the trailer hand valve while moving may result in:

answers.text: the trailer to disconnect.
a skid on the trailer.
answers.explanation: Application of the trailer hand valve while moving may cause the trailer to skid from the brakes applying too fast. Only use the trailer hand valve for testing the brakes during pre-trip inspection.

answers.text: loss of air pressure to all vehicle brakes.

explanation: Application of the trailer hand valve while moving may cause the trailer to skid from the brakes applying too fast. Only use the trailer hand valve for testing the brakes during pre-trip inspection.

question: Any of the following combinations may roll over, which one is MOST likely to roll over?

answers.text: The rear trailer of a double combination
The rear trailer of a triple combination
answers.explanation: The most rearward vehicle in a combination vehicle configuration is most likely to roll over. Each additional trailer increases the chance.

answers.text: The front trailer of a triple combination

explanation: The most rearward vehicle in a combination vehicle configuration is most likely to roll over. Each additional trailer increases the chance.

question: The tractor protection valve will close, and the trailer emergency brakes will come on when there is a major leak in the ____ brake line.

answers.text: Parking
Service
Emergency
answers.explanation: If all the air in the trailer is lost to a leak, the emergency brakes will be activated. Ensure that both air lines to the trailer are connected correctly and not leaking.

explanation: If all the air in the trailer is lost to a leak, the emergency brakes will be activated. Ensure that both air lines to the trailer are connected correctly and not leaking.

question: Which of the following wheels will result in the greatest amount of off-tracking?

answers.text: The rear axle wheels of the front trailer
The rear drive wheels of the tractor
The rear axle wheels of the rear trailer
answers.explanation: Off-tracking will always affect the wheels on the most rearward axle of your combination vehicle.

explanation: Off-tracking will always affect the wheels on the most rearward axle of your combination vehicle.

question: Which statement is true of air supply to the trailer brakes?

answers.text: The trailer air supply valve controls the tractor protection valve
answers.explanation: The trailer air supply control is a red 8-sided knob on newer vehicles that supplies air pressure to the trailer when pushed in. The trailer air supply control also controls the tractor protection valve.

answers.text: The trailer air supply valve on newer vehicles is a yellow knob
Pushing in the trailer air supply control knob will shut off air pressure to the trailer brakes.

explanation: The trailer air supply control is a red 8-sided knob on newer vehicles that supplies air pressure to the trailer when pushed in. The trailer air supply control also controls the tractor protection valve.

question: There are two things that a driver can do to prevent a rollover: They are: (1) go slow around turns, and (2):

answers.text: Keep the cargo as close to the ground as possible.
answers.explanation: Rollover prevention is primarily the result of going slow through turns (slowing momentum) and keeping the cargo as low to the ground as possible (lowering the center of gravity).

answers.text: Keep both hands firmly on the steering wheel.
Make sure that the brakes are properly balanced.

explanation: Rollover prevention is primarily the result of going slow through turns (slowing momentum) and keeping the cargo as low to the ground as possible (lowering the center of gravity).

question: Where in the vehicle should cargo be loaded to avoid a rollover?

answers.text: Load the front of the trailer to the top and then work backward to maximize storage space.
Distribute the cargo evenly through the entire trailer.
answers.explanation: By evenly distributing the cargo throughout the trailer the center of gravity will be the lowest, resulting in the least chance of rollover.

answers.text: Load cargo on the axles first, to the top of the trailer, and then the rest evenly distributed.

explanation: By evenly distributing the cargo throughout the trailer the center of gravity will be the lowest, resulting in the least chance of rollover.

question: Which of the following will confirm that all air brakes on each trailer are getting an air supply?

answers.text: Going to the rear of the front trailer and opening the air supply line and checking for air venting.
Ensuring that the air tank pressure is 100 psi or greater.
Going to the rear of the last trailer and opening the service line and emergency line shut-off and listening for air venting
answers.explanation: Regardless of the number of trailers, go to the rear of the last trailer. Open the service line and emergency line shut-off and listen for air venting to the atmosphere. If air cannot be heard, there is a problem in the system.

explanation: Regardless of the number of trailers, go to the rear of the last trailer. Open the service line and emergency line shut-off and listen for air venting to the atmosphere. If air cannot be heard, there is a problem in the system.

question: When coupling, the proper position of the fifth wheel is:

answers.text: Tilted down toward the end of the trailer.
answers.explanation: When backing the tractor up to the trailer the 5th wheel should be positioned so that it is tilted up toward the end of the trailer.

answers.text: Level of the ground.
Tilted up toward the end of the trailer.

explanation: When backing the tractor up to the trailer the 5th wheel should be positioned so that it is tilted up toward the end of the trailer.

question: There are two things that a driver can do to prevent a rollover. They are: (1) keep the cargo as close to the ground as possible and (2):

answers.text: Make sure that the brakes are properly adjusted.
Go slow around turns.
answers.explanation: Rollover prevention is primarily the result of going slow through turns (slowing momentum) and keeping the cargo as low to the ground as possible (lowering the center of gravity).

answers.text: Keep both hands firmly on the steering wheel.

explanation: Rollover prevention is primarily the result of going slow through turns (slowing momentum) and keeping the cargo as low to the ground as possible (lowering the center of gravity).
